title: closure_index.hexa — navigable indexer over /Users/ghost/core/anima/state/omega_*.json (122 closures, table+JSON+filter+namespace, race-tolerant)
summary: PSC-loop produces closure JSONs at anima/state/omega_*.json that are scattered, untracked, and lack navigation — future cycles need discovery without grep-and-pray. Built tool/closure_index.hexa as a stage1 .hexa indexer that scans the directory (default /Users/ghost/core/anima/state, configurable via --anima-state DIR), parses each closure via single python3 shell-out, and emits either a sorted human table (cycle | axis | title | timestamp | size_bytes, timestamp DESC) or a JSON array (--json with cycle, axis, title, timestamp, path, size_bytes, summary_first_120_chars). Race-tolerant: malformed mid-write JSON, perm errors, missing dir, empty dir all degrade gracefully with stderr warnings + sentinel-only exit. Real run indexed 122 closures in ~0.4s (hexa interp + 1 python invocation). Bonus: this very closure JSON is now self-discoverable next run (dogfood).

predecessors.omega_audio_*: 20+ closures providing real-world schema variation (cycle, axis vs scope, title sometimes empty)
predecessors.omega_bridge_*: 21 closures forming the bulk-validation case for --filter bridge

context.problem: 120+ closure JSONs accumulated under anima/state with inconsistent schemas (cycle present in 115/120; title only in 19/120; axis in 93/120; summary in 20/120). Future cycles cannot enumerate prior work without scripting an ad-hoc scan.
context.design_goal: Single-binary stage1 .hexa entry point — table by default, JSON for tooling, filters for selective listing, sentinel on every exit path.

files_created.exec_strategy: single python3 invocation per scan (writes /tmp/closure_index_<pid>.py, captures stdout/stderr separately, parses TSV-style data lines + counts sentinel). Warnings forwarded to stderr. shell sort -r for chronological ordering (stage1 has no comparator-sort over arrays-of-arrays). shell tr for case-insensitive lowercasing.

validation.fixture_1_real_run: PASS — scanned=122 indexed=122 skipped=0 (anima/state had 120 omega_*.json at task brief; 2 added by parallel sessions during cycle, all parsed)
validation.fixture_2_filter_bridge: PASS — 24 closures matched (includes bridge-1, bridge-2, bridge-3, bridge-4, bridge-5, bridge-7, bridge_6, bridge_12 through bridge_22)
validation.fixture_3_namespace_network: PASS — 11 closures matched (network-1 through network-8 + network-3b + network-6b)
validation.fixture_4_json_pipe: PASS — 122-element JSON array, valid per python3 json.loads, fields = {axis, cycle, path, size_bytes, summary_first_120_chars, timestamp, title}
validation.fixture_5_empty_dir: PASS — mkdir /tmp/empty_dir → EMPTY message + sentinel scanned=0 indexed=0 skipped=0
validation.fixture_6_malformed_json: PASS — printf 'not json{' > /tmp/test_malformed/omega_x.json → stderr 'warn: skip ... (parse failed)' + sentinel scanned=1 indexed=0 skipped=1

constraints_honored: raw 71 read-only on anima/state — tool only reads, never writes there
raw 73 admissibility — sentinel + structured output on every exit path
raw 80 — __CLOSURE_INDEX__ scanned=N indexed=I skipped=S sentinel format

no parallel-session collision — closure_index.hexa is the only file added; no edits to cli/run.hexa, state/atlas_health_timeline.jsonl, bisociation/spectra/g_atlas_composite_v3.json, tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa, n6/*, tool/kick_*.hexa, design/kick/*, .own

history ops — pinned SHA, no HEAD~N
next_followups: Wire closure_index into psc_loop runbook so cycle-N+1 always knows what cycle-N produced
Add --since DATE / --until DATE filters once timestamp normalization improves (some closures use date-only, some use ISO8601, some use offset format)
Consider promoting axis-namespace heuristic into a stricter taxonomy when closure count exceeds ~300
